steps.title: Use custom Desmos regression for b
steps.content: The equation form is already given: $y=36x^2-bx-120$. Since the table shows an x-intercept at $(6,0)$, enter $x_1=[6]$ and $y_1=[0]$, then type the custom regression row $y_1 \sim 36x_1^2-bx_1-120$. Desmos solves the unknown coefficient and returns $b=196$.

steps.title: Solve for b
steps.content: A quick hand check matches the regression: substitute $x=6$ and $y=0$ into $y=36x^2-bx-120$ to get $0=36(6)^2-6b-120=1176-6b$. Thus $6b=1176$ and $b=196$. The value of $b$ is 196.
